name: KrillApp.DataPoint.Filter
title: Filters
description: A container node that visually organizes data filtering rules under a DataPoint. Does not filter data itself — actual filtering is performed by individual filter nodes (DiscardAbove, DiscardBelow, Deadband, Debounce) when the owning DataPoint ingests a value. Parent/child grouping is visual organization only: a filter takes effect only when the DataPoint lists it in its meta.inputs, and every listed filter must pass for a snapshot to be stored.
shortDescription: Container for filter nodes; a filter takes effect when listed in the owning DataPoint's inputs.
llmPurpose: Visually organizes data filter nodes under a DataPoint. Filters listed in the DataPoint's meta.inputs are evaluated when the DataPoint ingests a value.
llmBehavior: filters listed in the owning DataPoint's meta.inputs are evaluated during ingest
all listed filters must pass for data to be stored
a filter merely parented here but absent from the DataPoint's inputs does nothing
llmInputs: none directly — individual filters receive snapshots during the DataPoint's ingest
llmOutputs: none directly — individual filters accept or reject snapshots
llmTypicalUseCases: add data quality filters to a data point
llmPromptHints: add a filter to this data point
llmCreationHints: Create a Filter node as a child of a DataPoint when the user wants to apply data quality or noise reduction rules. Then add specific filter types and list each one in the DataPoint's meta.inputs — parenting alone does nothing.

llmConnectionHints.role: organizational container between a DataPoint and specific filter implementations; no execution semantics of its own
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Not wired — grouping is visual only; each filter must be listed in the owning DataPoint's meta.inputs to participate in snapshot validation.

llmExamples: Add filters to this data point to clean up noisy data.
Set up data quality rules for this sensor.
I want to filter out bad readings from this data point.
